resource_id.description: Optional ID of specific resource instance. Omit to check general permission for resource type

resource_type.description: Type of resource to check access for (e.g. 'Agent', 'MCPServer')

authorized.description: Whether the user is authorized to perform this action

resource_id.description: Optional ID of specific resource instance. Omit to check general permission for resource type

resource_type.description: Type of resource to check access for (e.g. 'Agent', 'MCPServer')

agent_id.description: The agent ID that owns the session

agent_session_id.description: The agent session ID to associate with the run

agent_id.description: The agent to use for this message

create_agent_session.description: Whether to create an agent session for this message

parent_message_id.description: Optional parent message ID, for regenerating messages

prompt.description: The prompt to send to the conversation

agent_session.description: The created agent session, if applicable

emails.description: List of email addresses to add to the team

input_variables.description: Input variables that are found as placeholders throughout the mcp server configuration (for example headers, env, args, etc)

is_internal.description: Indicates if this is a backend-generated server (true) or user-defined (false)

think.description: Text block that represents the model's thought process.

access_checks.description: List of resource-action pairs to check access for

results.description: Authorization results for each access check

served_from_cache.description: whether this response was served from cache

description.description: describes the content for the model, in natural language. Can use markdown formatting

history.description: Describes the supplied context for user, will be visible in history

kind.description: well-known values here (anything but "dynamic") opts in to specific behaviour

mime_type.description: the mime-type of the content. Must be a non-binary format

source.description: indicates the source of this context

content.description: content in the format specified by mime_type

last_message_at.description: Date when last message was added to the conversation

parent_message_id.description: ID of another message, referenced by some prompt_types (e.g. implement)

stop_reason.description: Set when the response is stopped due to a stop word being reached

task_id.description: ID of the task, if applicable

tool_operation_id.description: ID of the tool operation, if applicable

data.description: The plaintext credential data to encrypt and store

credential_id.description: The ID of the credential to use for authentication

default_branch.description: The default branch of the repository

remote_url.description: The remote URL of the repository

text.description: how the item will be displayed to the user

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.
examples: Property foo is required but is missing.

errors.description: Optional list of individual error details

instance.description: A URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence of the problem.

title.description: A short, human-readable summary of the problem type. This value should not change between occurrences of the error.

type.description: A URI reference to human-readable documentation for the error.

location.description: Where the error occurred, e.g. 'body.items[3].tags' or 'path.thing-id'

value.description: The value at the given location

get.description: List details for all agent sessions

post.description:
Start a new agent session.

When a new session is started a set of API credentials are created.
If the session is a local session, the requestor's identity will be used.
If the session is a remote session, the agent's configured identity will be used.
